NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND A ~20 MILE SWATH OF 70 - 80 MPH WINDS WHICH CAUSED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE. THE DAMAGE STARTED AT TAMARACK PARK AND CONTINUED SOUTHEAST TO LONG LAKE AND EMERSON. POWER POLES WERE DESTROYED ALONG MAPLE ROAD LOCATED BETWEEN THE LAKES. THE SWATH OF TREE DAMAGE CROSSED US-10 BY EMERSON LAKE AND EXTENDED AN UNKNOWN DISTANCE TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF US-10. START TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
BUOY STATION 45024, LUDINGTON BUOY.
TREES DOWN ON STRUCTURES AROUND BRANCH TOWNSHIP. US-10 PARTIALLY BLOCKED. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. SOME DAMAGE TO BARNS AND HOUSES.

AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAX WINDS OF 85 MPH CAUSED TREE AND PROPERTY DAMAGE IN THE VILLAGE OF BALDWIN. PATH LENGTH AROUND 1 MILE. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH AROUND 500 YARDS. A SECOND TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN LAKE COUNTY AROUND ONE-MILE TO THE SOUTHEAST OF THE END OF THE TRACK OF THIS TORNADO...NEAR IDLEWILD.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS 60 TO 70 MPH WINDS DOWNED MULTIPLE TREE BRANCHES PRIOR TO THE EF0 TORNADO IN BALDWIN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SEVERAL 8 INCH DIAMETER TREES REPORTED DOWN NEAR 12 MILE RD AND SHANER AVE.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAX WINDS OF 100 MPH CAUSED TREE AND PROPERTY DAMAGE JUST EAST OF THE VILLAGE OF BALDWIN NEAR IDLEWILD. PATH LENGTH AROUND 1 MILE. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF AROUND 500 YARDS. THIS TORNADO WAS THE SECOND TORNADO PRODUCED FROM THIS STORM AND TOUCHED DOWN JUST UNDER ONE MILE FROM THE END OF THE TRACK OF THE PREVIOUS TORNADO.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN LILLEY TWP. SOME ROADS INACCESSIBLE.
TREES AND POWER LINES REPORTED DOWN FROM THE LUDINGTON, SCOTTVILLE, CUSTER, AND FOUNTAIN AREAS.
MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN ACROSS REED CITY.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN AREA OF TREE DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH UP TO 75 MPH WIND GUSTS SOUTHEAST OF BROHMAN ALONG 6 MILE ROAD NEAR EVERGREEN DRIVE EXTENDING TO SPRUCE AVENUE. SPOTTY AND MUCH LESS INTENSE DAMAGE CONTINUED SOUTHEASTWARD TO APPROXIMATELY PINE AVENUE AND EAST 3 MILE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED A SMALL SWATH OF INTENSE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED AROUND WELLS LAKE. DAMAGE ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE LAKE WAS IMMENSE WITH A NARROW SWATH OF NEARLY ALL TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. WEST OF THE LAKE, DAMAGE WAS SOMEWHAT LESS WITH SMALLER TREE BRANCHES SNAPPED OR DOWNED. THE DAMAGE IS CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 70 TO 80 MPH. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
DELAYED REPORT. ISOLATED TREES DOWN ACROSS ALLEGAN COUNTY FROM STORMS TUESDAY EVENING. TIME ESTIMATED AT ALLEGAN FROM RADAR.

DAMAGE FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS ESTIMATED AT 75-90 MPH STRETCHED FROM PARIS TO BIG RAPIDS TO STANWOOD. NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES AND DAMAGE TO PROPERTY FROM FALLING TREES, WITH WORST DAMAGE CONCENTRATED IN PARIS AND STANWOOD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR BETWEEN 749 PM AND 802 PM.
TREES DOWN BLOCKING ROADS.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN THE AREA, SOME BLOCKING ROADS. TREES CRUSHED A FEW CAMPER TRAILERS.

*** 1 INJ *** NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES AROUND HARRISON WITH ONE BARN DESTROYED. ONE HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES. A PERSON WAS INJURED AFTER A TREE FELL ON THEIR CAR WITH THEM INSIDE. EXACT LOCATION OF DAMAGE POINTS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 55 TO 65 MPH DOWNED LARGE TREE BRANCHES WITH A FEW UPROOTED TREES FROM WINDOVER LAKE TO LAKE GEORGE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
PHOTOS OF NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND SOME SNAPPED. DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AND VEHICLES DUE TO FALLEN TREES. AFFECTED A LARGE PORTION OF LEOTA AREA.
A DOZEN REPORTS ACROSS THE COUNTY OF TREES ACROSS ROADS. ONE VEHICLE ACCIDENT DUE TO A FALLEN TREE.
TREE DOWN AT INTERSECTION OF 15 MILE ROAD AND M-60. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREES DOWN AT INTERSECTION OF AB AVENUE AND DOUGLAS AVENUE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH 75 MPH WINDS WAS CONFIRMED AT THE NORTH END OF LITTLE LONG LAKE. THE PATH WAS ESTIMATED AT 0.4 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. SEVERAL TREES AND LIMBS WERE SNAPPED. A HOUSE WAS DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES.
TREE DOWN ON NORTH BROADWAY STREET JUST NORTH OF THE BRANCH COUNTY LINE IN UNION CITY. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH. PATH WIDTH APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 0.75 MILES. ONE PREFAB HOME DESTROYED WITH NUMEROUS TREES DAMAGED. LARGE PROPANE TANK AND LARGE WOOD OVEN DRAGGED AROUND 20 FEET.
UNOCCUPIED TRAILER HOME DESTROYED NEAR INTERSECTION OF WASHINGTON ROAD AND 137TH AVENUE. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 65-80 MPH CONFIRMED NEAR JACKSON, MI. PATH ESTIMATED AT 1.5 MILES LONG WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 225 YARDS. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AND SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO HOUSES WAS DUE TO FALLING TREES AND LIMBS.
LOCATION IS THE MID POINT OF A 3.8 MILE DAMAGE PATH. TREES DOWN AND SOME ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS. PATH ESTIMATED AT 4 MILES THOUGH BULK OF DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE NORTHEAST 1.5 MILES OF THE PATH WITH INTERMITTENT DAMAGE CONFINED TO HILLTOPS ALONG THE REMAINDER OF THE PATH. SEVERAL HOMES HAD ROOF DAMAGE AND AN OLDER BARN STRUCTURE WAS SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED.
